title: Add a new tutorial for using Buildah with registries

comments.body: Looks like Travis is having trouble reaching the fedora repos again (???)
@ipbabble I wish it were "again", it's "still".  Fedora registry has been baked for the most part since Saturday at least.
Oh and be sure to triple check "docker" to Docker throughout unless it's used in a command example.

@ipbabble Looks like you included a Merge Branch. 

git rebase -i origin
git push --force

Should fix it.
Docker is always capitalized unless it is a Docker CLI reference like `docker run`.  That is what is says on their usage page.
Test issue is an infrastructure problem not related to this PR.  I'm going to try to retest.
ok, bot's not listening to me.  Going to close and reopen to respin the tests.

two small b's to two big B's and I'm hip with merging.  @nalind any concerns with merging if Red Hat CI is still off the rails?  This submit is just documentation, no working bits and the failure isn't related.
Thanks for all the nits Tom. Of course my frustrations with git spells mean every time I see a Tom nit I have to think, "I must be thankful Tom finds nit. I must be Thankful Tom finds nits ...."
LGTM, thanks @ipbabble!  Green lights, let's merge this bad boy!

:pushpin: Commit 4d94375 has been approved by `nalind`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=nalind 4d943752fef667b8ce68f7ca7d144fab0da8b176 -->
Bot's not doing its thing, merging manually.
@ipbabble Can you also submit this tuturial to the Projectatomic blog to be published there.  We can get more eyeballs.
We should wait until #353 is fixed before publishing it there.

Ack. I planned to but Im waiting for Josh Berkus.
